jokes.text: You know that model in all them Hugo Boss ads? - Yeah. - You don't look nothing like him.
jokes.setup: Will asks if Carlton knows the Hugo Boss model, implying a complimentary comparison
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's actually an insult - Carlton looks nothing like the handsome model
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection with efficient setup. The pause creates perfect comedic timing, leading audience to expect a compliment before the reversal.

jokes.text: You've never even found a woman you'd share a burrito with.
jokes.setup: Carlton claims he's found the woman to share his life with
jokes.punchline: Will reduces it to Carlton's inability to share even a simple burrito
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-based humor that contrasts Carlton's grandiose romantic claims with a mundane food-sharing reality. The specificity of 'burrito' makes it memorable.

jokes.text: Sure it wasn't your breath?
jokes.setup: Carlton gives a pseudo-psychological explanation for why Cindy left him
jokes.punchline: Will offers a much simpler, more embarrassing explanation
jokes.explanation: Simple insult humor that's effective but not particularly creative. The brevity works well as a deflating response.

jokes.text: Oh, look, hors d'oeuvres. Isn't it great to be rich?
jokes.setup: Will notices fancy appetizers
jokes.punchline: Direct acknowledgment of the family's wealth with casual enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's fish-out-of-water perspective and appreciation for luxury, delivered with his characteristic directness.

jokes.text: Is Daddy bringing home white people?
jokes.setup: Carlton is dressed formally
jokes.punchline: Ashley assumes it must be for white guests
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary delivered through a child's innocent observation about the family's code-switching behavior.

jokes.text: Door! Door! Door!
jokes.setup: Carlton is nervously waiting for Cindy
jokes.punchline: His manic repetitive shouting shows his anxiety
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy showing Carlton's nervous energy. Simple but effective character moment.

jokes.text: All right, don't wet yourself. I'll get it.
jokes.setup: Carlton's frantic door behavior
jokes.punchline: Will's crude but accurate assessment of Carlton's state
jokes.explanation: Will's typical blunt response to Carlton's overwrought behavior, delivered with perfect timing.

jokes.text: Say hello to Daddy.
jokes.setup: Cindy has returned to see Carlton
jokes.punchline: She's brought a baby that she claims is Carlton's
jokes.explanation: Major plot twist delivered with perfect economy. The audience and Carlton are equally shocked by this revelation.

jokes.text: Congratulations, man. He's a boy. Carlton, come on, man.
jokes.setup: Carlton has fainted from shock
jokes.punchline: Will treats it like Carlton just gave birth
jokes.explanation: Clever role reversal joke where Will treats the unconscious Carlton as if he just delivered the baby himself.

jokes.text: Carlton, a father? He won't even let Ken and Barbie sleep in the same shoebox.
jokes.setup: The idea of Carlton being sexually active
jokes.punchline: Reference to his extreme prudishness with toys
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character joke that perfectly captures Carlton's prudishness through a specific, absurd detail about keeping toy dolls separated.

jokes.text: Hey, the longest relationship Carlton ever had was with last year's... Ebony swimsuit issue.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Carlton's romantic history
jokes.punchline: His most committed relationship was with a magazine
jokes.explanation: Standard 'guy with magazine' joke elevated by the specific reference to Ebony and Carlton's character.

jokes.text: Well, it looks like they wear the same size.
jokes.setup: Question about whether the baby looks like Carlton
jokes.punchline: Will focuses on their similar small stature
jokes.explanation: Clever way to mock Carlton's small size while avoiding directly answering whether the baby resembles him.

jokes.text: Oh, he is so cute. Oh! Get this drool machine away from me.
jokes.setup: Hillary coos over the cute baby
jokes.punchline: Immediately disgusted when the baby drools on her
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hillary moment showing her superficiality - loves the baby until it inconveniences her designer outfit.

jokes.text: Well, I can assure you, it won't be me. This time, the butler didn't do it... hasn't done it... and I'd be surprised if he even remembered how to do it.
jokes.setup: Will's comment about someone getting in trouble
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's elaborate denial about his own sexual activity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Geoffrey moment combining the 'butler did it' trope with self-deprecating humor about his age and celibacy. The escalating denial structure is perfect.

jokes.text: Philip, I am never wearing a bathing suit again, as long as I live. Now, honey, I'm sure the pool man didn't really mistake you for me.
jokes.setup: Vivian is upset about a pool incident
jokes.punchline: The pool man confused her for her large husband
jokes.explanation: Fat joke at Philip's expense through the lens of mistaken identity. Functional but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: He called me Mr. Banks. That's still no reason to hold his head underwater, sweetheart.
jokes.setup: Pool man mistook Vivian for Philip
jokes.punchline: Vivian's violent overreaction to the mistake
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Vivian's fierce pride and Philip's reasonable response to her extreme reaction.

jokes.text: I'll get you some antacid for that. - No, don't. That wasn't me.
jokes.setup: Sound of apparent stomach trouble
jokes.punchline: It's actually the baby making the noise
jokes.explanation: Simple mistaken identity gag involving baby sounds. Functional but predictable.

jokes.text: Well, I'd take a shot in the dark and say around nine months.
jokes.setup: Vivian asks how long it's been since they saw Cindy
jokes.punchline: Will connects the timeline to pregnancy duration
jokes.explanation: Smart observational humor connecting the obvious timeline with typical pregnancy duration.

jokes.text: Now, for those of you just tuning in to Name That Father... this little bundle of joy belongs to a young man... who's seen Little Mermaid eight times.
jokes.setup: The dramatic baby revelation
jokes.punchline: Will frames it as a game show and reveals Carlton's childish movie preferences
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-comedy that breaks the fourth wall while delivering character-specific humor about Carlton's innocent nature. The game show format is inspired.

jokes.text: Not you, stupid. Sit your butt down.
jokes.setup: Vivian says they need to talk in private
jokes.punchline: She clarifies she means Carlton, not Will
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection with Vivian's typically sharp tone. Shows her priorities in the crisis.

jokes.text: Come on. Pep it up, little grey man.
jokes.setup: Will is clearing people out
jokes.punchline: His dismissive description of Geoffrey
jokes.explanation: Will's casual but affectionate nickname for Geoffrey shows their relationship dynamic.

jokes.text: Where do you think you are, Fantasy Island?
jokes.setup: Cindy claims she didn't mean to cause trouble
jokes.punchline: Philip references the TV show about impossible wishes coming true
jokes.explanation: Dated pop culture reference that works for the era but relies on external knowledge for the humor.

jokes.text: Carlton is the most incredible lover I have ever had... It was amazing, and endless, and magnificent. I think I saw God.
jokes.setup: Cindy claims to love Carlton
jokes.punchline: Her over-the-top description of Carlton as a lover
jokes.explanation: The absurd exaggeration creates cringe comedy, especially knowing Carlton's true inexperience. The 'saw God' line pushes it to ridiculous extremes.

jokes.text: The windows. I'm sorry. I fell. We didn't mean to. I fell. I fell.
jokes.setup: Cindy's graphic description of her and Carlton's alleged intimacy
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey and Will's physical reaction - apparently falling or fainting
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy reaction to the uncomfortable sexual revelation. The repeated 'I fell' suggests they were trying to escape through windows.

jokes.text: Or at least had you neutered.
jokes.setup: Philip wishes they had counseled Carlton about sex
jokes.punchline: Suggests extreme prevention method treating Carlton like a pet
jokes.explanation: Dark humor from the typically proper Philip, showing his desperation. The pet analogy is both shocking and perfectly timed.

jokes.text: You know... it's like the Rodney King videotape, you know? I just keep seeing it over, and over, and over again.
jokes.setup: Will is traumatized by something he witnessed
jokes.punchline: Compares it to the infamous, repeatedly-shown Rodney King footage
jokes.explanation: Topical reference that captures Will's trauma while connecting to current events. The repetition emphasizes his distress.

jokes.text: No, I'm not in trouble, but, no, check it out. Somebody we know is a grandma. No, not you, Mom. Mom. No, Mom. Mom, stop crying.
jokes.setup: Will calls his mother with news
jokes.punchline: His mother's misunderstanding and emotional reaction
jokes.explanation: One-sided phone conversation humor showing Will's mother jumping to conclusions. The escalating confusion is relatable.

jokes.text: Carlton is the father. Stop laughing, Mom.
jokes.setup: Will clarifies Carlton is the father
jokes.punchline: His mother's immediate laughter at the absurd idea
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction beat showing even Will's mother finds Carlton being sexually active laughably impossible.

jokes.text: That makes them bring it faster.
jokes.setup: Will says 'I love you' to end the pizza order call
jokes.punchline: His practical explanation for the affection
jokes.explanation: Clever insight into customer service psychology delivered with Will's street-smart practicality.

jokes.text: So when will your mommy and daddy be home?
jokes.setup: Philip angrily threatening Cindy's parents on the phone
jokes.punchline: Realizes he's been talking to a child the whole time
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and deflation. Philip's righteous anger completely deflated by the innocent revelation. Perfect comedic structure.

jokes.text: I never knew having a family could be so neat.
jokes.setup: Carlton playing with the baby and Cindy
jokes.punchline: His innocent, childlike description of adult responsibility
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton dialogue showing his naivety about serious adult situations. The word 'neat' is perfectly childish.

jokes.text: All right, whoa. I'd like to take this opportunity... to bring it to everyone's attention that Uncle Phil's head is about to explode... and, for once, it ain't my fault.
jokes.setup: Tense family scene with Philip angry
jokes.punchline: Will's commentary on Philip's anger and his own innocence
jokes.explanation: Great meta-commentary on the show's typical dynamic where Will is usually in trouble. The formal announcement format adds to the humor.

jokes.text: Yeah, it smells like you needs to be changed, too.
jokes.setup: Carlton saying the baby needs to be changed
jokes.punchline: Will suggests Carlton also needs changing (personality/behavior)
jokes.explanation: Double meaning joke playing on 'needs to be changed' - works for both baby diapers and Carlton's character.

jokes.text: Hold me back, Philip. - Carlton, get out. Get out, now. Go. Hey, I got him, but take another step, and I'll fill you full of potassium.
jokes.setup: Carlton asks if his parents will financially support his family
jokes.punchline: Vivian's rage and Will's bizarre threat involving potassium
jokes.explanation: Absurdist humor with Will's completely nonsensical threat. The randomness of 'potassium' makes it memorably bizarre.

jokes.text: Are you climbing down Rapunzel's extensions?
jokes.setup: Carlton appears to be climbing down from a window
jokes.punchline: Will's reference mixing fairy tale with modern hair extensions
jokes.explanation: Creative blend of fairy tale reference with contemporary hair fashion. The visual of Carlton climbing is inherently funny.

jokes.text: No, Carlton, nobody drives to Lompoc. You drive through Lompoc.
jokes.setup: Carlton says they're driving to Lompoc
jokes.punchline: Will's dismissive description of the town as a pass-through
jokes.explanation: Geographic humor suggesting Lompoc is so unremarkable no one would intentionally go there.

jokes.text: Yeah, you lucky I ain't going to do you like Kunta Kinte and chop off your foot.
jokes.setup: Carlton says his word should be enough
jokes.punchline: Will's extreme reference to preventing escape from Roots
jokes.explanation: Dark humor referencing the famous miniseries, but relies heavily on external cultural knowledge and is somewhat harsh.

jokes.text: Perhaps you'd like me to catch a Frisbee in my teeth?
jokes.setup: Philip giving Geoffrey commands
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's sarcastic suggestion treating him like a dog
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey sarcasm highlighting his mistreatment. The specific image of catching a Frisbee is both absurd and pointed.

jokes.text: Look, Adolph, Eva, why don't you all relax for a second.
jokes.setup: Philip and Vivian being overly suspicious and controlling
jokes.punchline: Will compares them to Hitler and Eva Braun
jokes.explanation: Extreme comparison to Nazi leaders for comedic effect. The reference is clear but relies on shock value.

jokes.text: Who knew Christmas would come twice this year?
jokes.setup: Geoffrey found $1.25 under Carlton's bed
jokes.punchline: His excited reaction to the small amount of money
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey's dry humor about being underpaid, treating pocket change like a windfall. Perfect character moment.

jokes.text: I don't know, Uncle Phil. Did you check the cracks in the couch?
jokes.setup: Philip asks where Carlton is
jokes.punchline: Will suggests looking in impossibly small spaces due to Carlton's size
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Carlton's small stature with a specific, absurd suggestion about where to find him.

jokes.text: That's a good idea. I'll call one, you call the other.
jokes.setup: Suggestion to call Carlton's friends
jokes.punchline: Philip implies Carlton only has two friends total
jokes.explanation: Efficient joke highlighting Carlton's social isolation. The casual delivery makes the insult funnier.

jokes.text: Here comes little Mr. Ducky. Doesn't he quack you up?
jokes.setup: Carlton trying to entertain the crying baby
jokes.punchline: His terrible pun combining 'crack up' with duck sounds
jokes.explanation: Intentionally bad pun that's funny because it's so Carlton-like. The joke works because of character context rather than cleverness.

jokes.text: If I could pass like that, they wouldn't make me play on the girl's team.
jokes.setup: Baby makes a noise (presumably gas)
jokes.punchline: Carlton admires the baby's 'passing' ability compared to his athletic inadequacy
jokes.explanation: Clever double meaning joke combining baby functions with Carlton's athletic failures. Self-deprecating humor that fits his character perfectly.

jokes.text: Yo, hey, look, my fault. You all, see, my cousin was supposed... Hey, congratulations, man. So you're having his baby?
jokes.setup: Will bursts into chapel to stop wedding
jokes.punchline: Officiant misunderstands and thinks Will is having Carlton's baby
jokes.explanation: Good confusion comedy with the officiant misunderstanding the dramatic situation completely.

jokes.text: She left me. I got the feeling she didn't want to live with me. - What made you think that? - She said she didn't want to live with me.
jokes.setup: Carlton explains Cindy left him with vague language
jokes.punchline: Reveals she was completely direct about not wanting to live with him
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery showing Carlton's tendency to overthink obvious situations. The structure is textbook setup/punchline.

jokes.text: Oh, man, I mean, she can't just walk out on you like that. What if the kid is yours? He's not. Carlton, come on, just because the baby is cute... doesn't mean you're not the father.
jokes.setup: Will suggests Carlton could still be father
jokes.punchline: Implies ugly people can have cute babies
jokes.explanation: Backhanded insult disguised as logic. Will's attempt to be helpful accidentally calls Carlton unattractive.

jokes.text: you a German?
jokes.setup: Carlton struggles to confess something embarrassing
jokes.punchline: Will's completely wrong guess about Carlton's confession
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection that completely deflates the serious moment. The randomness of 'German' is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: It's nice to know somebody still is.
jokes.setup: Carlton confesses he's a virgin
jokes.punchline: Officiant's world-weary comment about virginity being rare
jokes.explanation: Nice understated reaction from a character who's presumably seen it all. The timing works well.

jokes.text: I mean, hey, don't feel bad though. I mean, a lot of guys are... you know, Germans.
jokes.setup: Will tries to comfort Carlton about being a virgin
jokes.punchline: Continues the 'German' euphemism from his earlier mistake
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Will doubling down on his mistake, creating a running euphemism for virginity.

jokes.text: no way. Yucky.
jokes.setup: Will explains his approach to sexual opportunities
jokes.punchline: Claims he rejects advances with childish language
jokes.explanation: Will's mock-innocent explanation using deliberately childish language to describe his supposed rejection of sex.

jokes.text: Good, 'cause I'm really looking forward to losing my accent.
jokes.setup: Will assures Carlton he won't be a 'German' forever
jokes.punchline: Carlton takes the euphemism literally
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that shows Carlton's literal interpretation of Will's metaphor. The accent reference completes the absurd German connection beautifully.
